title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
summary: Military historian Sarah Paine presents a two-thousand-year geopolitical framework dividing global powers into continental empires pursuing security through territorial conquest and maritime powers pursuing prosperity through trade. The lecture explains how this divide shaped Russia, China, Britain, and the US, and how the Industrial Revolution altered these dynamics.
writeup: Military historian Sarah Paine lays out a two-thousand-year framework that splits global powers into two types: continental empires that pursue security through territorial conquest, and maritime powers that pursue prosperity through trade. She applies the distinction to Russia, China, Britain, and the United States, tracing how geography pushed each toward one strategy or the other. The lecture also addresses how the Industrial Revolution shifted these dynamics. The core takeaway is that the continental versus maritime divide offers a durable lens for reading the strategic choices of major states across centuries.
